title: Tangy Argentinian Tripe Stew
description: A tangy and velvety stew featuring tripe, perfect for a weeknight dinner, with a high protein content to satisfy any appetite. This traditional Argentinian dish is cooked to perfection in an earthenware bowl.

ingredients.name: beef tripe
ingredients.notes: cleaned and cut into small pieces

instructions: Preheat the oven to 300 degrees Fahrenheit.
Brown the chopped onion and minced garlic in a large skillet over medium heat.
Add the cleaned and cut tripe to the skillet and cook until browned on all sides.
Transfer the tripe mixture to an earthenware bowl and add the roast beef broth, diced tomatoes, argentinian chimichurri, red pepper flakes, salt, and black pepper.
Cover the earthenware bowl with a lid and transfer it to the preheated oven.
Bake the stew for 2 hours, or until the tripe is tender and the sauce has thickened.
Remove the stew from the oven and let it cool for a few minutes before serving.
Serve the stew hot, garnished with fresh parsley or oregano if desired.
